daily_brief: The Middle East war dominated global headlines as Israel struck Tehran during Nowruz celebrations while Iran continued attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure, hitting Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi refinery [France 24, BBC, Globe and Mail]. Prime Minister Netanyahu claimed Iran is being 'decimated' and confirmed Israel acted alone in striking Iran's South Pars gas field, with President Trump requesting no further attacks on energy targets [BBC, Le Monde]. The energy crisis deepened as Strait of Hormuz traffic dropped 95%, prompting Canada, Japan, and European allies to signal willingness to help reopen the waterway [BBC, Globe and Mail]. The U.S. Treasury floated the extraordinary possibility of lifting sanctions on some Iranian oil to ease market pressures [BBC]. In Europe, reports emerged that Denmark had prepared to destroy Greenland airport runways in the event of a U.S. invasion, underscoring transatlantic tensions [BBC, Financial Times]. Meanwhile, Belarus released 250 political prisoners in a U.S.-brokered deal [Le Monde], and the EU summit failed to secure Hungary's support for a €90 billion Ukraine loan [RFI]. Relief agencies warned the conflict could push 45 million additional people into acute hunger by midyear [Globe and Mail].

top_stories.headline: Israel Strikes Tehran on Nowruz as Iran Hits Gulf Energy Sites, Escalating Regional Conflict
bullets: Israeli airstrikes hit Tehran on Friday as Iranians marked Persian New Year; Iran launched multiple missile barrages at Israel throughout Thursday and Friday
An Iranian drone struck Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi oil refinery, causing fires and shutting down some units; heavy explosions were reported over Dubai as air defenses intercepted incoming fire
Netanyahu stated Israel 'acted alone' in the South Pars gas field strike and confirmed Trump requested no further attacks on energy infrastructure
Iran has executed three men, including teenager Saleh Mohammadi, convicted over January anti-government protests in the first hangings related to the demonstrations

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on Netanyahu's statements and diplomatic fallout with U.S.

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized broader regional impact and AI-generated propaganda concerns

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Netanyahu's 'decimated' rhetoric and Iran's continued missile capabilities

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Stressed economic implications and Israel's agreement to refrain from further gas field strikes

top_stories.headline: Global Energy Crisis Deepens as Strait of Hormuz Traffic Falls 95% and U.S. Weighs Sanctions Relief
bullets: Daily ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has dropped from approximately 138 vessels to 5-6 per day since the war began February 28
Brent crude reached $113 per barrel; oil-producing nations released 400 million barrels from strategic reserves to offset supply disruptions
U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessent floated lifting sanctions on Iranian oil already at sea to ease market pressures, drawing sharp criticism from sanctions experts
Canada, Japan, UK, France, Germany, Italy, and the Netherlands issued a joint statement expressing readiness to contribute to efforts ensuring safe passage through the Strait

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed shipping data analysis and vessel origins

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized allied diplomatic coordination and Canadian involvement

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Featured former EU commissioner warning of 20-day window before severe consequences

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Focused on airline jet fuel shortage fears

top_stories.headline: Denmark Prepared to Destroy Greenland Runways in Event of U.S. Invasion, Reports Reveal
bullets: Danish soldiers deployed to Greenland in January were prepared to blow up key airport runways if the U.S. attempted to invade the Arctic island
Blood supplies were brought in to treat potential wounded; Copenhagen sought political support from France, Germany, and Nordic nations
The report, based on 12 sources in Danish government and military plus European allies, was confirmed by two European officials to the Financial Times
Danish defence ministry declined to comment; both the U.S. and Denmark are NATO members

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting emphasizing NATO alliance tensions

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Same coverage, highlighting European solidarity efforts

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Confirmed report through independent European official sources

top_stories.headline: Belarus Releases 250 Political Prisoners in Largest U.S.-Brokered Deal in Years
bullets: Belarus freed 250 political prisoners Thursday following negotiations with U.S. envoy John Coale, who met President Lukashenko in Minsk
Released prisoners include human rights activists Valentin Stefanovich, Marfa Rabkova, and Anastasiya Loika
In exchange, the U.S. agreed to remove sanctions on several Belarusian companies
Opposition leader Svetlana Tikhanovskaya thanked Trump while urging release of hundreds still detained since disputed 2020 elections

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Balanced coverage noting Trump's diplomatic approach toward Russia ally while acknowledging humanitarian milestone

top_stories.headline: EU Summit Fails to Break Hungary's Block on €90 Billion Ukraine Loan
bullets: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban refused to lift his veto on the €90 billion EU loan to Ukraine at Brussels summit
Orban is leveraging the loan dispute over damage to an oil pipeline running through Ukraine that has reduced Russian oil flow to Hungary and Slovakia
EU diplomat stated 'no movement from Orban' and acknowledged the block is linked to Hungary's April 12 elections
EU High Representative Kaja Kallas warned it is 'really, really time' to show support for Ukraine by unlocking funding

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Orban's electoral motivations and EU frustration

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Broader context of Europe's energy crisis and summit priorities

additional_stories.headline: Relief agencies warn 45 million additional people could face acute hunger by midyear due to Middle East war's impact on food and fuel prices
Venezuela's interim President Delcy Rodriguez replaces all senior military commanders following U.S.-backed ouster of Maduro
EU agrees to review emissions trading system as energy crisis from Middle East war puts pressure on climate policies
UK bilateral aid to African countries to be cut by 56% (nearly £900m) by 2028-29 to fund increased defense spending
Japan pledges $109 billion in U.S. investment and financing, the largest commitment among countries reaching trade deals with Trump
Singapore's ship-fueling hub faces soaring prices and tightening supply due to Middle East crisis, raising regional logistics concerns
Cyprus president calls for 'frank discussion' on UK military bases, describing them as 'colonial consequence' after drone attack
Umberto Bossi, founder of Italy's Northern League party and longtime populist figure, dies at 84
World Happiness Report finds heavy social media use linked to declining well-being among teenagers, particularly girls in Western countries
